color-surface-base.usage: Use as the background for the primary content area, cards, modals, and any surface that sits at the base elevation. Do not use for text or interactive elements.
color-surface-base.design: First step in the surface elevation scale. Anchors the layering system — raised surfaces sit above this token, not below.

color-surface-raised.summary: Elevated surface background, one step above the base layer.
color-surface-raised.usage: Use for cards, panels, and drawers that sit above the base page surface. Do not use for the root page background or inline content areas.
color-surface-raised.design: Second step in the surface elevation scale. Creates visual separation from surface-base through a subtle warm-grey tint.
